overview: Niv to Light is a five-color midrange toolbox deck that leverages Bring to Light to access a wide array of silver bullets and threats tailored to the metagame. Its core engine revolves around Niv-Mizzet Reborn to generate massive card advantage and apply relentless pressure, supported by ramp like Sylvan Caryatid and a rebuilt mana base for consistency. The deck aims to stabilize early with efficient removal and board wipes, then snowball into a late-game state where chaining Nivs and using Kolaghan's Command for reach closes out games.

keep_criteria: Hands with a turn 1 land and turn 2 Sylvan Caryatid or Growth Spiral
Hands containing Bring to Light or Niv-Mizzet Reborn with adequate mana
Hands with early removal like Dreadbore or Abrupt Decay
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (including fetchable basics or Cornucopia), at least one early game piece (Yorion, Cornucopia, or a cheap cantrip/removal), and a win condition or setup tool.
Prioritize hands with Cornucopia + cantrips for consistency.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early ramp or mana fixing
Hands with only high-cost threats and no lands
Hands missing Bring to Light in a format where toolbox consistency is key
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or hands with only late-game threats and insufficient mana/spells to cast them.

examples.reason: Provides turn 1 land, turn 2 ramp, and access to the deck's core engine and removal.
Lacks early ramp and mana sources to deploy threats efficiently, leading to a stalled start.

early_game: Play a triome or basic land on turn 1 to fix mana and enable turn 2 plays.
Cast Sylvan Caryatid or Growth Spiral on turn 2 to accelerate to four mana and block small creatures.
Use early removal like Dreadbore or Abrupt Decay to answer aggressive threats.
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a cantrip or deploy Cornucopia.
Turn 2: Develop board with Yorion or cheap creatures/spells, fix mana with Cornucopia fetches, and start casting cheap instants/sorceries to build Niv's storm count.
If missing Yorion/Cornucopia, prioritize drawing into them via cantrips.
mid_game: Deploy Niv-Mizzet Reborn to draw cards and apply flying pressure.
Utilize Bring to Light to find specific silver bullets like Shadows' Verdict or Slaughter Games based on the matchup.
Cast Expressive Iteration on turn 3 or later to filter for threats and answers.
Hoard instants and sorceries for Niv-Mizzet Reborn.
Use Cornucopia to fix mana and fuel Niv's ability, but avoid sacrificing it prematurely if you need the mana for stabilization.
Don't overcommit Yorion if the opponent has sweepers in hand.
Manage your graveyard carefully if facing hate cards, though Niv's primary engine is hand-based.
Commit Yorion early to establish board presence and trigger card draw, but hold back Niv until you have a clear path to win or enough instant/sorcery density.
late_game: Chain Niv-Mizzet Reborn into itself to overwhelm opponents with card advantage.
Use Kolaghan's Command to rebuy threats and generate reach.
Play board wipes like Deafening Clarion or Solar Blaze to reset the board and swing life totals.
Niv-Mizzet Reborn copying key instants/sorceries for a storm win or massive value, or Yorion out-valuing through repeated triggers.
Ancient Cornucopia can be sacrificed to cast a game-winning spell or fuel Niv's ability.
Once Niv is on the board with 3+ instants/sorceries in hand/graveyard, you can often win in one turn.
Go all-in when you have 3+ instants/sorceries ready, the opponent has no sweepers, and you can safely cast Niv.
Also commit Yorion when you can draw into answers or finishers.
If the opponent is tapped out or has no interaction, cast Niv immediately and chain spells to win.

key_cards.name: Bring to Light
key_cards.notes: Allows access to the entire deck, enabling silver bullets and shifting deckbuilding week-to-week to address specific matchups.

key_cards.name: Niv-Mizzet Reborn
key_cards.notes: A 6/6 flyer that draws 2-4 cards on ETB, making 1-for-1 trades highly favorable and enabling a chaining engine.

key_cards.name: Sylvan Caryatid
key_cards.notes: Best pure ramp card in Pioneer; accelerates from two to four mana, fixes mana, blocks 2-power creatures, and survives removal.

key_cards.name: Dreadbore
key_cards.notes: Handles planeswalkers and creatures alike; allows double-spelling and holds up instant-speed spells post-board.

key_cards.name: Kolaghan's Command
key_cards.notes: Rebuys creatures, answers smaller threats, and provides the deck's only form of reach in game 1.

key_cards.name: Expressive Iteration
key_cards.notes: Draws cards and filters the deck; best played on turn 3 or later to ensure two cards and target specific threats.

sequencing_tips: Play Expressive Iteration on turn 3 or later to guarantee drawing two cards and better filter for late-game answers.
Use Growth Spiral to search for lands only when necessary, ideally after ensuring a free land drop to trigger Omnath's ability.
Double-spell with Dreadbore and another removal spell to efficiently clear threats while holding up instant-speed interaction.
Prioritize casting Sylvan Caryatid on turn 2 over Growth Spiral for better blocking and survival against removal.
Save Deafening Clarion for when you have a Niv, Omnath, or Scarab God in play to maximize life gain swings.
Common mistakes include overcommitting Yorion into sweepers, failing to hoard instants/sorceries for Niv, and mismanaging Cornucopia's tap ability versus its sacrifice ability.
Unique interactions: Cornucopia can be sacrificed to cast Niv's ability or other spells, enabling storm combos.
Niv's ability copies spells even if they target it, so protect it but don't fear targeting.
Always track opponent's hand for sweepers/counters, and remember that Yorion's triggers can be chained with cheap creatures to draw into game-winning pieces.

matchups.advice: Use lifegain from Omnath and Deafening Clarion to stall, deploy The Scarab God to lock out recursion, and use board wipes to clear small creatures.
Bring to Light for Slaughter Games to instantly deal with combo pieces, and use Shadows' Verdict to wipe boards and graveyards.
Leverage Solar Blaze to handle larger threats, use Kolaghan's Command for reach, and prioritize chaining Niv-Mizzet Reborn for card advantage.
Apply pressure with Niv-Mizzet Reborn, use Vanishing Verse for mono-colored threats, and filter for answers with Expressive Iteration.

Use cheap spells to probe for counterspells, cast Niv/Yorion in response to counterspells if possible, or use Cornucopia to cast around them. Sideboard in more uncounterable threats or extra interaction. If heavily countered, pivot to Yorion value and out-grind the opponent.

Apply early pressure with Yorion, disrupt mana with discard/removal, and use Cornucopia to outpace their mana development. Don't let them stabilize; keep casting spells and trading efficiently. Sideboard in more early interaction if needed.

Win the resource war. Prioritize card advantage (Yorion), protect Niv, and sideboard in targeted hate. Trade efficiently and out-value in the late game. Control the board with removal and use Cornucopia to fuel Niv faster than your opponent.

common_threats: Watch for board wipes (sweepers), targeted removal on Niv/Yorion, and combo win conditions.
Note if the opponent is playing around Niv (counterspells, discard, or graveyard hate).
Track their mana base and hand for sweepers or hard counters.
Pay attention to when they tap out for value vs. when they hold up interaction.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Powerful midrange strategies can shift answers and threats to respond to any top deck... One hallmark of Niv to Light is using Bring to Light to enable a suite of silver bullets... The deck’s second namesake, Niv-Mizzet Reborn, acts as this deck’s card advantage engine and primary win condition.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Niv-Mizzet Reborn, acts as this deck’s card advantage engine and primary win condition.
Omnath, Locus of Creation gaining you life and presenting a blocker that’s difficult to answer
The Scarab God... locks out aggro decks and the rebuying of threats like Niv or Omnath often locks up any close game.
Valki, God of Lies... get a 7-mana planeswalker that’s incredibly hard to answer.
Kolaghan's Command allows you to rebuy your creatures in the late game... this is your only form of reach in game 1.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: You want to play this on turn 3 or later to make sure you get two cards out of the deal.
ideally wait until you can ensure a free land before you cast it.
it’s easy to double-spell with Dreadbore and another piece of removal at just .
there really isn’t any debate on which you’d rather cast on turn 2 in a format littered with small creatures.
Deafening Clarion works well at clearing smaller creatures off the board while giving huge swings of life if you already have a Niv, Omnath, or Scarab God in play.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Except for cards like Esika's Chariot or Jeskai Ascendancy, Dreadbore can handle it all.
Many decks overload on 3-drop creatures like Archon of Emeria that can cause issues.
Tolsimir, Friend to Wolves is a backbreaking answer to small aggressive creatures.
A silver bullet against other midrange decks and decks that use the graveyard for recursive creatures.
Between mono green, Boros burn being exclusively red permanents, mono black aggro, and vampires, along with plenty of other important mono-colored cards
